preamble: The diphthongization of a nasalized open vowel [ã] to [ai] or [ãi] is often a feature of dialectal variation or historical development in languages with strong nasal vowel systems.

examples.note: Dialectal variation where the nasalized vowel [ã] is produced as a diphthong [ãi].

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
